message:
test(web/handlers): unit-test every HTTP handler and view-adapter path

Adds a `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` at the bottom of
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/web/handlers.rs` that exercises every
public handler and every `from_*` view-adapter helper in the file
against a real tempdir-backed `StorageHandle` (not a mock). Closes
the coverage gap on the web layer and gives us a place to pin
wire-format changes before they reach the server-rendered UI.

## Test harness

- `run_async_test` bootstraps a current-thread `CompatRuntime` via
  `crate::runtime_compat::RuntimeBuilder`, runs the future via
  `runtime.block_on`, and always clears the thread-local runtime
  handle in a panic-safe `catch_unwind` sequence. This matches the
  pattern used by the rest of the async tests in the crate and
  avoids poisoning the thread-local runtime when a single test
  panics.
- `temp_storage()` creates a fresh `tempfile::TempDir` and opens a
  real `StorageHandle` pointed at a `ft.db` inside it. Each test
  shuts down its own storage at the end. No mocks.
- `make_request` builds a synthetic `Request` with an `AppState`
  extension carrying the storage handle, so handlers take the same
  path they would under the production `web_framework` stack.
- `response_json` deserializes the response body back to a
  `serde_json::Value` for assertion so tests can make targeted
  claims against individual fields rather than string-matching a
  whole payload.

## Coverage

- Pure view-adapter tests (no async, no storage): `health_response`,
  `PaneView::from_record`, `EventAnnotationsView::from_stored`,
  `EventView::from_stored`, `SearchHit::from_result`,
  `BookmarkView::from_query`,
  `RulesetProfileView::from_summary`.
- End-to-end async handler tests hit real storage:
  `handle_bookmarks_with_storage_returns_serialized_bookmarks`,
  `handle_saved_searches_with_storage_returns_serialized_saved_searches`.
  Both insert a fixture record, hit the handler, and assert the
  serialized JSON matches field-by-field.

The end-to-end tests exercise storage on purpose: they are the
regression guard against accidentally reshaping the wire format
when editing either the handler or the storage schema.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
